backtrace.c(3) Library Functions Manual backtrace.c(3)

NAME

backtrace.c - 📍 Backtrace capture, symbolization, and formatting implementation

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void backtrace_capture (backtrace_t *bt)
void backtrace_symbolize (backtrace_t *bt)
void backtrace_capture_and_symbolize (backtrace_t *bt)
void backtrace_t_free (backtrace_t *bt)
void backtrace_print_simple (int skip_frames)
void backtrace_print (const char *label, const backtrace_t *bt, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)
void backtrace_print_many (const char *label, const backtrace_t *bts, int count)
int backtrace_format (char *buf, size_t buf_size, const char *label, const backtrace_t *bt, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)
void backtrace_log_frames (int num_frames, int skip_frames)

Detailed Description

📍 Backtrace capture, symbolization, and formatting implementation

Definition in file debug/backtrace.c.

Function Documentation

void backtrace_capture (backtrace_t * bt)

18   if (!bt) {
19     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid backtrace");
20     return;
21   }
22   bt->count = platform_backtrace(bt->ptrs, 32);

  backtrace_capture_and_symbolize (backtrace_t * bt)

int
  backtrace_format (char * buf, size_t buf_size, const char * label, const
  backtrace_t * bt, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t
  filter)


Definition at line 363 of file
  debug/backtrace.c.


364                                                                       {
365   if (!buf || buf_size == 0 || !bt || !bt->symbols || bt->count <= 0) {
366     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
367     return -1;
368   }
369 
370   // Capture count and symbols once to avoid TOCTOU race with other threads
371   int count = bt->count;
372   char **symbols = bt->symbols;
373 
374   int offset = 0;
375 
376   // Add label
377   offset += safe_snprintf(buf + offset, buf_size - (size_t)offset, "%s\n", label);
378 
379   // Calculate frame limits
380   int start = skip_frames;
381   int end = count;
382   if (max_frames > 0 && (start + max_frames) < end) {
383     end = start + max_frames;
384   }
385 
386   // Add frames
387   int frame_num = 0;
388   for (int i = start; i < end && i < count && offset < (int)buf_size - 128; i++) {
389     // Safely access symbols[i] - symbols array might be smaller than count or have NULL entries
390     const char *symbol = NULL;
391     if (symbols && symbols[i]) {
392       symbol = symbols[i];
393     } else {
394       symbol = "???";
395     }
396 
397     // Skip frame if filter says to
398     if (filter && filter(symbol)) {
399       continue;
400     }
401 
402     offset += safe_snprintf(buf + offset, buf_size - (size_t)offset, "  [%d] %s\n", frame_num, symbol);
403     frame_num++;
404   }
405 
406   return offset;
407 }






References safe_snprintf().






void
  backtrace_log_frames (int num_frames, int skip_frames)


Definition at line 409 of file
  debug/backtrace.c.


409                                                            {
410   backtrace_t bt;
411   backtrace_capture_and_symbolize(&bt);
412   backtrace_print("Backtrace", &bt, skip_frames, num_frames, NULL);
413   backtrace_t_free(&bt);
414 }






References backtrace_capture_and_symbolize(),
    backtrace_print(), and backtrace_t_free().






void
  backtrace_print (const char * label, const backtrace_t * bt, int skip_frames,
  int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)


Definition at line 72 of file debug/backtrace.c.


73                                                       {
74   if (!bt || !bt->symbols || bt->count <= 0) {
75     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid backtrace params");
76     return;
77   }
78 
79   // Capture count and symbols once to avoid TOCTOU race with other threads
80   int count = bt->count;
81   char **symbols = bt->symbols;
82 
83   // Calculate frame limits
84   int start = skip_frames;
85   int end = count;
86   if (max_frames > 0 && (start + max_frames) < end) {
87     end = start + max_frames;
88   }
89 
90   // Build backtrace in two versions: colored for terminal, plain for log file
91   char colored_buffer[16384] = {0};
92   char plain_buffer[16384] = {0};
93   int colored_offset = 0;
94   int plain_offset = 0;
95 
96   // Format log header using logging system's template
97   char log_header_buf[512] = {0};
98   time_t now = time(NULL);
99   struct tm *tm_info = localtime(&now);
100   char timestamp[32];
101   strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%H:%M:%S", tm_info);
102 
103   thread_id_t tid = asciichat_thread_self();
104   uint64_t tid_val = (uintptr_t)tid;
105 
106   // Get current time in nanoseconds for template formatting
107   uint64_t time_ns = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() * 1000ULL;
108 
109   // Try to format header using the logging system's template with color
110   log_template_t *format = log_get_template();
111   if (format) {
112     // Color the label with WARN color for terminal output
113     const char *colored_label_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, label);
114     char colored_label_buf[256];
115     strncpy(colored_label_buf, colored_label_ptr, sizeof(colored_label_buf) - 1);
116     colored_label_buf[sizeof(colored_label_buf) - 1] = '\0';
117 
118     int len = log_template_apply(format, log_header_buf, sizeof(log_header_buf), LOG_WARN, timestamp, __FILE__,
119                                  __LINE__, __func__, tid_val, colored_label_buf, true, time_ns);
120     if (len > 0) {
121       // Successfully formatted with logging template
122       colored_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_buffer + colored_offset, sizeof(colored_buffer) - (size_t)colored_offset,
123                                       "%s\n", log_header_buf);
124     } else {
125       // Fallback: manual formatting if template fails
126       safe_snprintf(log_header_buf, sizeof(log_header_buf), "[%s] [WARN] [tid:%llu] %s: %s", timestamp, tid_val,
127                     __func__, label);
128       const char *colored_header_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, log_header_buf);
129       char colored_header_buf[512];
130       strncpy(colored_header_buf, colored_header_ptr, sizeof(colored_header_buf) - 1);
131       colored_header_buf[sizeof(colored_header_buf) - 1] = '\0';
132       colored_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_buffer + colored_offset, sizeof(colored_buffer) - (size_t)colored_offset,
133                                       "%s\n", colored_header_buf);
134     }
135   } else {
136     // Fallback: manual formatting if no template available
137     safe_snprintf(log_header_buf, sizeof(log_header_buf), "[%s] [WARN] [tid:%llu] %s: %s", timestamp, tid_val, __func__,
138                   label);
139     const char *colored_header_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, log_header_buf);
140     char colored_header_buf[512];
141     strncpy(colored_header_buf, colored_header_ptr, sizeof(colored_header_buf) - 1);
142     colored_header_buf[sizeof(colored_header_buf) - 1] = '\0';
143     colored_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_buffer + colored_offset, sizeof(colored_buffer) - (size_t)colored_offset,
144                                     "%s\n", colored_header_buf);
145   }
146 
147   // Add plain label header for log file
148   plain_offset +=
149       safe_snprintf(plain_buffer + plain_offset, sizeof(plain_buffer) - (size_t)plain_offset, "%s\n", label);
150 
151   // Build backtrace frames with colored output for terminal, plain for log
152   int frame_num = 0;
153   for (int i = start; i < end && i < count && colored_offset < (int)sizeof(colored_buffer) - 512; i++) {
154     // Safely access symbols[i] - symbols array might be smaller than count or have NULL entries
155     const char *symbol = NULL;
156     if (symbols && symbols[i]) {
157       symbol = symbols[i];
158     } else {
159       symbol = "???";
160     }
161 
162     // Skip frame if filter says to
163     if (filter && filter(symbol)) {
164       continue;
165     }
166 
167     // Build frame number string
168     char frame_num_str[16];
169     safe_snprintf(frame_num_str, sizeof(frame_num_str), "%d", frame_num);
170 
171     // Get colored frame number - copy to temp buffer to avoid rotating buffer issues
172     const char *colored_num_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, frame_num_str);
173     char colored_num_buf[256];
174     strncpy(colored_num_buf, colored_num_ptr, sizeof(colored_num_buf) - 1);
175     colored_num_buf[sizeof(colored_num_buf) - 1] = '\0';
176 
177     // Parse symbol to extract parts for selective coloring
178     // Format: "[binary_name] function_name() (file:line)"
179     char colored_symbol[2048] = {0};
180     const char *s = symbol;
181     int colored_sym_offset = 0;
182 
183     // Color binary name between brackets
184     if (*s == '[') {
185       colored_sym_offset +=
186           safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "[");
187       s++;
188       // Find closing bracket
189       const char *bracket_end = strchr(s, ']');
190       if (bracket_end) {
191         int bin_len = bracket_end - s;
192         char bin_name[512];
193         strncpy(bin_name, s, bin_len);
194         bin_name[bin_len] = '\0';
195         const char *colored_bin_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, bin_name);
196         char colored_bin_buf[512];
197         strncpy(colored_bin_buf, colored_bin_ptr, sizeof(colored_bin_buf) - 1);
198         colored_bin_buf[sizeof(colored_bin_buf) - 1] = '\0';
199         colored_sym_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset,
200                                             sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "%s", colored_bin_buf);
201         colored_sym_offset +=
202             safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "] ");
203         s = bracket_end + 1;
204       }
205     }
206 
207     // Skip leading spaces after bracket
208     while (*s && *s == ' ')
209       s++;
210 
211     // Parse: could be "function() (file:line)" or "file:line (unresolved)"
212     const char *paren_start = strchr(s, '(');
213 
214     // Detect format: if there's a colon before the first paren, it's "file:line (description)"
215     const char *colon_pos = strchr(s, ':');
216     if (colon_pos && paren_start && colon_pos < paren_start) {
217       // Format: "file:line (unresolved function)" - rearrange to "(unresolved) (file:line)"
218       // Extract file:line part (trim trailing spaces)
219       int file_len = paren_start - s;
220       while (file_len > 0 && s[file_len - 1] == ' ')
221         file_len--;
222       char file_part[512];
223       strncpy(file_part, s, file_len);
224       file_part[file_len] = '\0';
225 
226       // Extract description content (without parens)
227       const char *paren_end = strchr(paren_start, ')');
228       int desc_content_len = paren_end - paren_start - 1; // -1 to skip opening paren
229       char desc_content[512];
230       strncpy(desc_content, paren_start + 1, desc_content_len); // +1 to skip opening paren
231       desc_content[desc_content_len] = '\0';
232 
233       const char *colored_desc_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, desc_content);
234       char colored_desc_buf[512];
235       strncpy(colored_desc_buf, colored_desc_ptr, sizeof(colored_desc_buf) - 1);
236       colored_desc_buf[sizeof(colored_desc_buf) - 1] = '\0';
237       colored_sym_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset,
238                                           sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "(%s)", colored_desc_buf);
239 
240       // Now color file:line in parens
241       // Skip leading spaces in file_part
242       const char *file_start = file_part;
243       while (*file_start && *file_start == ' ')
244         file_start++;
245 
246       char *file_colon = strchr(file_start, ':');
247       if (file_colon) {
248         int filename_len = file_colon - file_start;
249         char filename[512];
250         strncpy(filename, file_start, filename_len);
251         filename[filename_len] = '\0';
252 
253         const char *colored_file_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, filename);
254         char colored_file_buf[512];
255         strncpy(colored_file_buf, colored_file_ptr, sizeof(colored_file_buf) - 1);
256         colored_file_buf[sizeof(colored_file_buf) - 1] = '\0';
257 
258         const char *line_num = file_colon + 1;
259         const char *colored_line_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, line_num);
260         char colored_line_buf[512];
261         strncpy(colored_line_buf, colored_line_ptr, sizeof(colored_line_buf) - 1);
262         colored_line_buf[sizeof(colored_line_buf) - 1] = '\0';
263 
264         colored_sym_offset +=
265             safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, " (%s:%s)",
266                           colored_file_buf, colored_line_buf);
267       }
268     } else if (paren_start) {
269       // Format: "function() (file:line)"
270       int func_len = paren_start - s;
271       char func_name[512];
272       strncpy(func_name, s, func_len);
273       func_name[func_len] = '\0';
274 
275       const char *colored_func_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEV, func_name);
276       char colored_func_buf[512];
277       strncpy(colored_func_buf, colored_func_ptr, sizeof(colored_func_buf) - 1);
278       colored_func_buf[sizeof(colored_func_buf) - 1] = '\0';
279       colored_sym_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset,
280                                           sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "%s()", colored_func_buf);
281 
282       // Find file:line in second set of parens
283       s = paren_start + 1;
284       while (*s && *s != '(')
285         s++;
286 
287       if (*s == '(') {
288         const char *file_paren_end = strchr(s, ')');
289         if (file_paren_end) {
290           colored_sym_offset +=
291               safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, " (");
292           int file_len = file_paren_end - s - 1;
293           char file_part[512];
294           strncpy(file_part, s + 1, file_len);
295           file_part[file_len] = '\0';
296 
297           // Skip leading spaces in file_part
298           const char *file_start = file_part;
299           while (*file_start && *file_start == ' ')
300             file_start++;
301 
302           char *colon_pos = strchr(file_start, ':');
303           if (colon_pos) {
304             int filename_len = colon_pos - file_start;
305             char filename[512];
306             strncpy(filename, file_start, filename_len);
307             filename[filename_len] = '\0';
308 
309             const char *colored_file_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, filename);
310             char colored_file_buf[512];
311             strncpy(colored_file_buf, colored_file_ptr, sizeof(colored_file_buf) - 1);
312             colored_file_buf[sizeof(colored_file_buf) - 1] = '\0';
313 
314             const char *line_num = colon_pos + 1;
315             const char *colored_line_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, line_num);
316             char colored_line_buf[512];
317             strncpy(colored_line_buf, colored_line_ptr, sizeof(colored_line_buf) - 1);
318             colored_line_buf[sizeof(colored_line_buf) - 1] = '\0';
319 
320             colored_sym_offset +=
321                 safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "%s:%s",
322                               colored_file_buf, colored_line_buf);
323           }
324           colored_sym_offset +=
325               safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, ")");
326         }
327       }
328     } else {
329       // No parens, likely a hex address - color with FATAL
330       const char *colored_addr_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, s);
331       char colored_addr_buf[512];
332       strncpy(colored_addr_buf, colored_addr_ptr, sizeof(colored_addr_buf) - 1);
333       colored_addr_buf[sizeof(colored_addr_buf) - 1] = '\0';
334       colored_sym_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset,
335                                           sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "%s", colored_addr_buf);
336     }
337 
338     // Format colored buffer: "  [colored_num] colored_symbol\n"
339     colored_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_buffer + colored_offset, sizeof(colored_buffer) - (size_t)colored_offset,
340                                     "  [%s] %s\n", colored_num_buf, colored_symbol);
341 
342     // Format plain buffer: "  [num] symbol\n"
343     plain_offset += safe_snprintf(plain_buffer + plain_offset, sizeof(plain_buffer) - (size_t)plain_offset,
344                                   "  [%d] %s\n", frame_num, symbol);
345     frame_num++;
346   }
347 
348   // Output backtrace through logging system (no direct fprintf to avoid ANSI codes in captured output)
349   log_warn("%s", plain_buffer);
350   log_file_msg("%s", plain_buffer);
351 }

    colored_string(), log_file_msg(), log_get_template(),
    log_template_apply(), platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), and

void
  backtrace_print_many (const char * label, const backtrace_t * bts, int
  count)


Definition at line 353 of file
  debug/backtrace.c.


353                                                                                 {
354   if (!bts || count <= 0) {
355     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: bts is NULL or count <= 0");
356     return;
357   }
358   for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
359     backtrace_print(label, &bts[i], 0, 0, NULL);
360   }
361 }






References backtrace_print().






void
  backtrace_print_simple (int skip_frames)


Definition at line 56 of file debug/backtrace.c.


56                                              {
57   void *buffer[32];
58   int size = platform_backtrace(buffer, 32);
59 
60   if (size > 0) {
61     char **symbols = platform_backtrace_symbols(buffer, size);
62 
63     // Skip platform_print_backtrace itself (1 frame) + any additional frames requested
64     backtrace_print_symbols("Backtrace", symbols, size, 1 + skip_frames, 0, NULL);
65 
66     platform_backtrace_symbols_destroy(symbols);
67   } else {
68     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to capture backtrace");
69   }
70 }






References platform_backtrace(),
    platform_backtrace_symbols(), and
    platform_backtrace_symbols_destroy().


Referenced by main().






void
  backtrace_symbolize (backtrace_t * bt)


Definition at line 27 of file debug/backtrace.c.


27                                           {
28   if (!bt || bt->tried_symbolize) {
29     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid symbolize params");
30     return; // Already tried or invalid (don't try twice)
31   }
32   bt->tried_symbolize = true;
33   bt->symbols = platform_backtrace_symbols(bt->ptrs, bt->count);
34 }






References platform_backtrace_symbols().

void
  backtrace_t_free (backtrace_t * bt)


Definition at line 45 of file debug/backtrace.c.


45                                        {
46   if (!bt) {
47     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid backtrace params");
48     return;
49   }
50   if (bt->symbols) {
51     platform_backtrace_symbols_destroy(bt->symbols);
52     bt->symbols = NULL;
53   }
54 }
